content.text:
The browser Supabase client isn't typed for the profile table, so I'll route through the proxy for onboarding checks instead.

[REDACTED]

All three issues are fixed. Here's what changed:

### 1. Register card overflow
The auth card used a fixed `520px` height with absolutely positioned faces, so the longer register form overflowed. The card now uses a CSS grid layout so both faces share the same cell and the container grows to fit the taller content. Fixed `min-height` values were removed, and the shell allows vertical scrolling on small screens.

### 2. Verification email popup
After successful registration:
- A modal appears: *"A verification email has been sent to your mail. Click the link in that email to verify your account."*
- The user is signed out (so they aren't sent to onboarding before verifying)
- After **5 seconds**, the modal closes, the form switches to login mode, and the user stays on `/auth`

### 3. Onboarding guard
Access control now checks `profile.onboarding_completed` (using the correct `profile` table with `user_id`):

- **`proxy.ts`** — For any route except `/`, `/auth`, and `/auth/callback`:
  - Unauthenticated users → `/auth`
  - Authenticated but not onboarded → `/onboarding`
  - Authenticated and onboarded on `/auth` → `/dashboard`
- **`dashboard/page.tsx`** — Server-side fallback redirect to `/onboarding`
- **`auth/page.tsx`** and **`auth/callback/route.ts`** — Redirect based on onboarding status
- **`onboarding/page.tsx`** — Fixed table name (`profiles` → `profile`)
- **`completeOnboarding()`** — Sets `onboarding_completed: true` when platform handles are saved

**Flow after login:**
- Onboarding incomplete → `/onboarding`
- Onboarding complete → `/dashboard`
- Home (`/`) remains accessible without onboarding

content.text:
Here’s what was updated:

### Form fields
- Removed **GitHub**, **HackerRank**, and **CodeChef** from the platform list
- Added **Name** (required) and **Description** (placeholder: `dsa enthusiast`)
- Kept **LeetCode**, **Codeforces**, and **Atcoder**

### Profile DB update
`completeOnboarding` now writes `name`, `description`, and `onboarding_completed` to the `profile` table (insert or update by `user_id`). The onboarding page also pre-fills name/description from the existing profile or auth metadata.

### Loading screen
After `completeOnboarding` succeeds, a full-screen overlay appears with a spinner and rotating messages:
- “Initialising your profile…”
- “Fetching your platform data…”

It stays visible until the `fresh-init` API call finishes, then redirects to the dashboard. If that API call fails, the overlay closes and an error is shown.

Checking how difficulty stats are computed — the arc lengths may not sum to a full circle.

[REDACTED]

Identifying issues:
1. Arc lengths use `d.total` as the denominator, but `total` can exceed `easy + medium + hard` (per analytics), leaving gaps.
2. Mismatched radii: background `r=40`, arcs `r=35` with circumference computed for `r=35`.
3. Floating-point rounding can leave small gaps between segments.

Applying the TopicDonut pattern: segment by difficulty sum, unified radius, and last segment fills the remainder.

[REDACTED]

input.description: Verify build passes after StatsOverview fix

content.text:
Three bugs were causing the incomplete donut:

### 1. Wrong denominator for arc lengths
Arcs were sized using `d.total` (from `solved_count`), but segments represent only `easy + medium + hard`. When some solved problems lack difficulty tags, `total` can be larger than the sum of the three — so the arcs never added up to a full circle.

**Fix:** Size arcs from `easy + medium + hard` only. The center label still shows `d.total`.

### 2. Mismatched radii
The background ring used `r=40` / `strokeWidth=13`, while the colored arcs used `r=35` / `strokeWidth=12`, with circumference calculated for `r=35`. That misaligned the rings.

**Fix:** All circles now share `r=40` and `strokeWidth=12`, with one shared circumference constant.

### 3. Floating-point gaps between segments
Each segment was computed independently, so tiny rounding errors could leave slivers between arcs.

**Fix:** Segments are built with a running offset (same approach as `TopicDonut.tsx`), and the last segment fills whatever remains of the circumference so the ring always closes.

The donut should now render as a complete circle whenever there is at least one tagged difficulty count.

content.text:
Two features were added to the Last 7 Days chart:

### Y-axis labels
Each horizontal grid line (at 25%, 50%, and 75% of the max value) now has a number to its left, computed as `Math.round(maxVal × fraction)`. The chart area was shifted slightly right to make room for these labels.

### Hover tooltips
Each data point has a larger invisible hit area. On hover:
- The point grows slightly
- A small tooltip appears above it showing that day's solved count
- The tooltip clears when the mouse leaves the chart

The chart logic was extracted into a `Last7DaysChart` subcomponent to keep hover state isolated from the rest of the stats grid.

input.description: Verify build after modal UX improvements

content.text:
Both overlays were improved for readability and proper popup behavior.

### Logout dialog
- Rendered via a **portal to `document.body`** so it always covers the full screen and centers correctly
- Replaced mixed auth-modal classes with dedicated **`dashboard-logout-*`** styles
- **Higher contrast text** — light title (`#f3f0f2`) and readable body copy (`#c8b8b2`) on a solid dark card
- **Clear button styling:**
  - **Cancel** — solid gray background (`#3a3a3f`), border, shadow
  - **Logout** — orange gradient background with border and hover lift
  - Both have min-height, bold text, cursor pointer, and visible shadows

### Onboarding loading overlay
- Also **portaled to `document.body`** so it behaves as a true fullscreen popup (not clipped/offset by the page layout)
- **Darker backdrop** with stronger blur
- **Prominent card** — solid light background, stronger border and shadow
- **Spinner in a circular badge** with a larger, more visible spinning ring animation
- **Explicit text colors** so title and subtitle stay readable on the card

Both modals now sit above everything (`z-index: 9999`) and should feel like proper confirmation/loading dialogs.

input.description: Verify build with new landing page

content.text:
A full landing page is now live at `/`.

### What's on the page

**Hero**
- Headline explaining AlgoMentor as a unified DSA / competitive programming tracker
- Primary CTA: **Get started free** → `/auth`
- Secondary CTA: **View dashboard** → `/dashboard`

**The problem**
- Scattered progress across platforms
- Invisible consistency and streaks
- Hidden weak topics

**Features**
- Unified platform sync (LeetCode, Codeforces, AtCoder)
- Streaks and activity tracking
- Difficulty, contest, and topic analytics
- Mentorship-focused progress view

**How it works**
1. Create an account  
2. Connect platform handles  
3. Open your dashboard  

**Footer CTA**
- **Log in / Sign up** → `/auth`
- **Go to dashboard** → `/dashboard`

### Navigation
The top bar includes **Log in**, **Sign up**, and anchor links to Features and How it works.

### Design
The page uses the same warm auth-page aesthetic and dark feature cards from the dashboard, with responsive layout and dark mode support.

Files added/updated:
- `app/components/LandingPage.tsx` — page content
- `app/page.tsx` — renders the landing page with metadata
- `app/globals.css` — landing page styles

The home route stays public in `proxy.ts`, so both logged-in and logged-out users can visit it. Unauthenticated users clicking **View dashboard** will be redirected to `/auth` by the existing auth guard.

<user_query>
how to setup the oauth callbacks for produion
</user_query>

I'll check how OAuth and callbacks are wired in this project so the production setup steps match your code.

[REDACTED]

Here’s how to set up OAuth callbacks for production in **this project** (Supabase + Google → `/auth/callback`).

## How your app works today

1. User clicks Google sign-in in `AuthPage.tsx`
2. Supabase redirects to Google
3. Google sends the user back to Supabase
4. Supabase redirects to your app at:

```text
https://<your-production-domain>/auth/callback
```

5. `app/auth/callback/route.ts` exchanges the `code` for a session and redirects to `/dashboard` or `/onboarding`

Your code already uses the right pattern:

```186:186:app/auth/AuthPage.tsx
                    redirectTo: `${location.origin}/auth/callback`
```

So in production, `location.origin` becomes your live domain automatically.

---

## 1. Supabase Dashboard (required)

Go to **[Supabase](https://supabase.com/dashboard) → your project → Authentication → URL Configuration**

| Setting | What to set |
|--------|-------------|
| **Site URL** | `https://your-domain.com` |
| **Redirect URLs** | Add both: `https://your-domain.com/auth/callback` and `http://localhost:3000/auth/callback` (for local dev) |

Also under **Authentication → Providers → Google**:
- Enable Google
- Add your Google **Client ID** and **Client Secret**

Supabase will show you its callback URL — it looks like:

```text
https://<project-ref>.supabase.co/auth/v1/callback
```

That is **not** your Next.js URL. Google redirects to Supabase first; Supabase then redirects to your app.

---

## 2. Google Cloud Console (required)

Go to **[Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/) → APIs & Services → Credentials → your OAuth client**

### Authorized JavaScript origins
```text
https://your-domain.com
http://localhost:3000
```

### Authorized redirect URIs
```text
https://<project-ref>.supabase.co/auth/v1/callback
```

Do **not** put `https://your-domain.com/auth/callback` in Google’s redirect URIs. That goes in Supabase’s **Redirect URLs**, not Google’s.

---

## 3. Production environment variables

On Vercel/your host, set:

```env
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=https://<project-ref>.supabase.co
NEXT_PUBLIC_ANON_KEY=<your-supabase-anon-key>
```

These are used in `browser-client.ts` and `server-client.ts`. Without them, OAuth will fail in production.

---

## 4. Deploy checklist

- [ ] App deployed at `https://your-domain.com`
- [ ] `/auth/callback` is reachable (your `proxy.ts` already skips it)
- [ ] Supabase **Site URL** = production domain
- [ ] Supabase **Redirect URLs** includes `https://your-domain.com/auth/callback`
- [ ] Google redirect URI = Supabase callback only
- [ ] Production env vars set and redeployed

---

## 5. Quick test flow

1. Open `https://your-domain.com/auth`
2. Click **Sign in with Google**
3. After Google login, you should land on:
   - `/onboarding` (new user), or
   - `/dashboard` (onboarding completed)

If it fails, you’ll be sent to `/auth?error=...` (handled in `AuthPage.tsx`).

---

## Common production mistakes

| Symptom | Likely cause |
|--------|----------------|
| Redirects to localhost after Google login | Supabase **Site URL** still set to `http://localhost:3000` |
| `redirect_uri_mismatch` from Google | Google redirect URI doesn’t exactly match `https://<ref>.supabase.co/auth/v1/callback` |
| Lands on `/auth` with no error | `https://your-domain.com/auth/callback` missing from Supabase **Redirect URLs** |
| OAuth works locally but not in prod | Production env vars missing or wrong Supabase project |

---

## Optional: preview/staging domains

If you deploy preview URLs (e.g. `https://dsa-mentor-abc.vercel.app`), add each to Supabase **Redirect URLs**:

```text
https://dsa-mentor-abc.vercel.app/auth/callback
```

Google OAuth for previews is trickier — you usually need a separate OAuth client or stick to email/password on preview deploys.

---

## Summary

```text
User → Google → Supabase (auth/v1/callback) → your app (/auth/callback) → dashboard/onboarding
```

You configure:
1. **Google** → Supabase callback URL  
2. **Supabase** → your app callback URL (`/auth/callback`)  
3. **Hosting** → Supabase env vars  

Your Next.js callback route is already set up; production is mostly Supabase + Google console configuration.

If you share your production domain (e.g. Vercel URL), I can list the exact URLs to paste into each dashboard.

[REDACTED]
